ksas.statement: Knowledge of program management frameworks (PMI, SAFe, Agile) and how they are adapted for data and AI programs that involve technical uncertainty, iterative development, and cross-functional dependencies.
Knowledge of data and AI program risk types, including technical risk, data availability risk, model performance risk, adoption risk, and regulatory compliance risk.
Knowledge of change management frameworks (Prosci ADKAR, Kotter) and how to adapt them for data and AI capability deployments with heterogeneous user populations.
Knowledge of adult learning design principles, including learning objectives, instructional design methods, knowledge retention research, and blended learning delivery models.
Knowledge of data literacy concepts, including reading and interpreting charts, understanding data sources, recognizing misleading visualizations, and applying basic statistical reasoning.

Knowledge of DataOps principles and practices, including pipeline monitoring approaches, automated alerting, SLA management, incident response workflows, and operational metrics for measuring data pipeline health and reliability.

Knowledge of IT service management principles as applied to data services, including service catalog design, service level management, incident and problem management, and continual service improvement.

Knowledge of technology vendor evaluation methodologies for data and AI platforms, including RFP and RFI processes, proof-of-concept design, total cost of ownership analysis, and vendor risk assessment.

Knowledge of data and AI operational budgeting practices, including cost allocation models, chargeback and showback frameworks, cloud cost optimization strategies, and ROI tracking for infrastructure investments.
Knowledge of self-service data platform principles, including data marketplace design, self-service analytics enablement, data product discovery mechanisms, access provisioning, and usage monitoring.
Skill in managing cross-functional data and AI programs, including workstream coordination, dependency management, stakeholder communication, and escalation.
Skill in designing data and AI training programs for diverse organizational audiences, from executives to operational staff, with role-appropriate content and delivery formats.
Skill in measuring adoption and identifying adoption barriers, including usage analytics review, stakeholder interviews, and friction point diagnosis.
Skill in translating technical data and AI concepts into accessible language and relatable examples for non-technical business audiences without sacrificing accuracy.
Skill in triaging data pipeline incidents, including failure identification, impact assessment, escalation decision, and status communication to affected consumers.

Skill in designing and operating data pipeline monitoring systems, including health dashboards, automated alerting configurations, SLA tracking, and anomaly detection for data freshness, completeness, and volume.
Skill in managing data and AI vendor relationships, including contract performance monitoring, issue escalation, renewal negotiation, and vendor consolidation planning.
Skill in conducting operational process improvement for data workflows, identifying inefficiencies, designing automation opportunities, and measuring improvement outcomes.
Skill in building and maintaining data service catalogs that document available data products, access methods, service level expectations, ownership, and support channels for data consumers.
Skill in facilitating data and AI communities of practice, including knowledge sharing forums, best practice documentation, cross-team collaboration initiatives, and capability building activities.
Develop and maintain program plans for data and AI initiatives, including scope definition, workstream structure, milestone schedule, resource plan, and risk register.
Manage program execution, including workstream coordination, issue resolution, scope change management, and executive status reporting.
Conduct program retrospectives and lessons learned documentation, producing findings that inform program management practice improvement.
Design, develop, and deliver data and AI training programs for defined organizational audiences, including needs assessment, curriculum development, delivery execution, and effectiveness measurement.
Manage the data and AI adoption program, including stakeholder engagement plans, adoption milestone tracking, barrier identification, and intervention deployment.
Develop and deliver data and AI literacy curriculum for non-technical organizational audiences, including module design, delivery facilitation, and effectiveness assessment.

Manage data pipeline operations, including monitoring execution schedules, investigating failures, coordinating remediation, verifying recovery, and documenting root cause analysis for recurring issues.
Conduct vendor evaluations for data and AI platforms, including requirements definition, proof-of-concept design and execution, technical and commercial assessment, and selection recommendation.
Manage operational budgets for data and AI programs, including cost forecasting, expenditure tracking, variance analysis, and cost optimization recommendations.
Develop and maintain operational documentation for data systems, including standard operating procedures, runbooks, escalation matrices, and knowledge base articles.
Manage data service level agreements with internal consumers, including SLA definition, performance measurement, breach reporting, and service improvement planning.
Conduct capacity planning for data infrastructure, including growth projection analysis, resource utilization trending, and scaling recommendations aligned with business demand forecasts.
Ability to balance competing operational priorities across data pipeline reliability, development velocity, cost constraints, and consumer experience requirements.
Ability to design operational processes that scale with organizational growth, maintaining service quality as data volume, consumer count, and pipeline complexity increase.
Ability to build cross-functional operational partnerships between data teams, IT infrastructure, business units, and vendor organizations to maintain effective data service delivery.
